name: Saturn
description: Saturn, in Roman mythology, was a complex and pivotal deity. He was initially an agricultural god, associated with sowing, harvest, and prosperity. Over time, he became identified with the Greek Titan Cronus, father of Zeus (Jupiter in Roman mythology). This association imbued Saturn with a more complex narrative, including his overthrow by his own son, Jupiter, after consuming his children to prevent the fulfillment of a prophecy that he would be dethroned. Saturn ruled during a mythical Golden Age, a time of peace, abundance, and equality before Jupiter's reign. After his defeat, Saturn fled to Italy, where he was welcomed by Janus and taught the people agriculture and civilization. He is often depicted with a sickle or scythe, representing his agricultural origins, and is associated with wealth, liberation, and periodic renewal. The festival of Saturnalia, a time of feasting, role reversal, and general merriment, was held in his honor each December. He represents a powerful figure of both destruction and creation, embodying the cyclical nature of time and the rise and fall of empires. His legacy is interwoven with themes of justice, order, and the consequences of power.
subtitle: God of Agriculture, Time, and Liberation

keyMyths.title: The Overthrow of Saturn
keyMyths.description: Saturn, fearing a prophecy, devoured his children. Rhea, his wife, hid Jupiter, who eventually overthrew Saturn.

keyMyths.title: Saturn's Flight to Italy
keyMyths.description: After being overthrown, Saturn fled to Italy, where he was welcomed by Janus and taught the people agriculture and civilization.

keyMyths.title: The Golden Age
keyMyths.description: Saturn ruled during a Golden Age of peace and abundance, a time before conflict and hardship existed.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A poem describing the Roman calendar and festivals, including Saturnalia.
sources.text: A poem describing the Roman calendar and festivals, including Saturnalia.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: An epic poem recounting the founding of Rome, mentioning Saturn's role in Italy.
sources.text: An epic poem recounting the founding of Rome, mentioning Saturn's role in Italy.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A Greek poem about farming and morality, describing the Golden Age under Cronus (Saturn).
